steps.instruction: Wipe and clean all working surfaces, including those of the PixCell IIe system, and tools (tweezers, forceps) as described above. Since the next step (microdissection) is to be carried out as soon as possible after section dehydration, this preparatory measures could be done while waiting for the sections to dehydrate in the last two jars (100% ethanol and xylene jars).

Place the incubation block (Arcturus Incubation Block for sample extraction from CapSure HS Caps, Applied Biosystems, CA, USA), which will be used to extract RNA once LCM is completed, inside dry heat bath with temperature set at 42 °C (Accublock™ digital dry bath, Labnet International Inc., Woodbridge, USA).

Section inspection and measurement : Use the stained sections which are designated to act as guide maps to identify the MCC zones through the LCM device, and create index-matched images to provide some guidance when microdissecting the unstained sections if needed. It is recommended to use the 100 µm grid to roughly measure the size of cells/zones of interest (Fig 2). Fig 2 Map images of stained MCC cryosections with 100µm grid overlay. (a) Under 4X objective. (b) Under 10X objective using HistoGene Staining Solution.
LCM cap (Arcturus CapSure HS LCM Caps, Applied Biosystems, CA, USA) is placed on the slide outside the sections area.
Under 10X objective, set the spot size to 7.5 µm and focus the IR laser as instructed by the manufacturer.
Then pulse the laser as a trial, this causes the polymer attached to the LCM cap to melt focally.

Adjust the laser power and duration up and down to get proper wetting of the polymer film, the melted spot should be seen as a clear circle surrounded by a sharp dark ring (Fig 3). Fig 3 (a) A properly melted polymer spot has a dark outer ring and a clear center. (b) Under-exposed polymer spot has a fuzzy appearance and lack the dark ring. (c) Laser over-exposure may lead to polymer burning.

Empirical setting of LCM parameters : Settings of the spot size, power or strength, and duration of the laser greatly vary according to the tissue type and size of the area to be dissected, the possibilities include microdissection of single cells, aggregates of cells, strips of tissue, or large areas of tissue. That’s why, empirically adjust the LCM parameters to obtain properly wetted polymer with the desired diameter. The suggested settings for fibrous zone (FZ) are 70-90mW power, 2-3ms duration with 7.5µm spot size. For the other three zones PZ, MZ, and HZ (Proliferative, Mature, and Hypertrophic chondrocytes zones), we suggest 45-65mW, 10-15ms, with 15µm spot size.

To perform microdissection, first review the saved map of the index matched section, place the section to be dissected on the stage of the LCM, locate the zone of interest, and start laser firing. To capture a continuous line, such as FZ, or an area, such as PZ, MZ, and HZ, fire the laser continually by pressing and holding the laser switch until the area of interest is entirely wetted by the polymer of the cap. Critical Step : Microdissect as many selected cells/areas per cap as possible by dissecting the six sections mounted on the same slide. CapSure HS LCM Cap is recommended as its 12 µm ridge keeps the polymer film elevated above the tissue section, minimizing nonspecific tissue adherence to the cap. However, the entire surface of the cap film can be used for LCM. NOTE: Documenting LCM : taking images for the tissue section before and after LCM, as well as images for the cap with the microdissected tissue is recommended.

When the mounted sections are large enough and allow homogenous isolation of cells from two zones, a second nonadjacent zone can be dissected. For instance FZ and MZ (or PZ and HZ) can be dissected from the same slide (the same six tissue sections) using two LCM caps, one for each zone. Critical Step : this multiple zones isolation process is performed in 30 ± 15 minutes including the dehydration step.

Apply PrepStrips (Arcturus PrepStrip™ Tissue Preparation Strip, Applied Biosystems, CA, USA) onto the section surface according to the manufacturer’s instructions. This is done to flatten the sections and to remove dust and loose tissue, hence, avoid nonspecific tissue adherence to the cap during LCM procedure.
